message:
fix(fcp-cli): Normalize arch names in target triple parsing for consistent artifact lookup

Target Triple Parsing Fix (install/mod.rs):
- Map x86_64 -> amd64 and aarch64 -> arm64 for consistent artifact naming
- Ensures install commands find the correct platform-specific binaries
- Aligns with common artifact naming conventions (Docker, GitHub releases, etc.)

Documentation:
- Update coverage-inventory.md to reflect new fcp-host integration test file
- Increment test count for subprocess connector integration tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -66,7 +66,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-3drg","title":"[IDEA-08] Retry Taxonomy + FCP Error Mapping","description":"# Retry Taxonomy + FCP Error Mapping

## Goal
Standardize retryability classification and mapping to FCP error taxonomy across connectors.

## Background / Why
Discord and Telegram each implement custom retry classifiers. Consolidating into SDK avoids divergence and improves consistency (Retry-After, backoff with jitter, terminal errors).

## Scope
- SDK-level `RetryDecision` types.
- Helper to map external errors into FCP error codes + retry hints.

## Deliverables
- `RetryDecision` enum (Immediate, Backoff, After(Duration), Terminal).
- `RetryPolicy` helper that translates `RetryDecision` into backoff and logs.
- Error mapping guidance in docs.

## Tests
- Unit tests for mapping correctness.
- Integration test with simulated HTTP errors (429/5xx/parse errors).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Connectors can classify errors without ad-hoc string matching.
- Retry behavior is consistent across connectors.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:35:45.362878825Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T19:36:21.618527516Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3drg","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:36:21.618496408Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3m2a","title":"TEST-CONNECTORS-LIVE: Real-service integration suites (manual)","description":"# TEST-CONNECTORS-LIVE: Real-service integration suites (manual)

## Goal
Add live integration tests for external services without mocks/fakes, using dedicated sandbox accounts.

## Scope
- Anthropic, OpenAI, Telegram, Discord, Twitter connectors.
- Real API calls with rate limits and secret handling.

## Constraints
- Must run as manual-only or gated by secrets in CI.
- No production accounts, no recording or fake fixtures.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each connector has a live test plan and minimal smoke tests.
- Logs redact secrets and include correlation IDs and cost/latency metadata.

 {"id":"bd-3oxr","title":"[IDEA-02.4] RateLimitDeclarations: end-to-end tests","description":"## Goal
Provide end-to-end coverage for rate limit declarations and enforcement.

## Details
- E2E scenario: connector advertises limits, host surfaces them, CLI renders them, and a throttled invoke returns structured retry metadata.
- Logging must include limit pool, remaining, reset timestamp.

## Tests
- E2E script with structured JSONL logs (depends on bd-11g2).
- Unit tests for error mapping and retry hints.

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E runner confirms rate limit metadata from manifest → host → CLI.
- Rate limited invokes include Retry-After and do not execute side effects.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:29:35.679594882Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T19:30:10.311084695Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3oxr","depends_on_id":"bd-11g2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:30:10.311053498Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3oxr","depends_on_id":"bd-1idx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:30:02.661476114Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3oxr","depends_on_id":"bd-328m","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:29:35.687274721Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3p99","title":"TEST: No-mock coverage + full E2E logging (FCP2)","description":"# TEST: No-mock coverage + full E2E logging (FCP2)

## Goal
Establish real-component unit/integration coverage (no mocks/fakes) plus a complete E2E script suite with rich structured logging.

## Definition: No mocks/fakes
- No mocking frameworks (wiremock), stub servers, or fake clients in the target suites.
- Tests exercise real code paths (actual protocol parsing, crypto, stores, mesh/session logic, sandbox enforcement).
- For external services (LLM, social APIs), tests must use dedicated sandbox accounts and real HTTP calls OR be marked manual-only with secrets gating.

## Deliverables
- Coverage inventory + gap map.
- Real-component test suites for core crates, mesh, store/repair, host+connector subprocess, sandbox/egress.
- E2E logging schema + validator + expanded scenario runner.

## Non-goals
- Do not delete existing mock-based tests; instead add real-component suites in parallel.
- No reliance on public production accounts.

## Success Criteria
- A coherent dependency graph of tasks that yields: (1) real-component unit/integration coverage, (2) comprehensive E2E scripts with machine-parseable logs.

Provide unit and integration tests for self-check support.

## Details
- Test connector implements self-check; host/CLI path returns expected output.
- Ensure timeouts and error mapping are handled.

## Acceptance Criteria

 {"id":"bd-3u58","title":"[IDEA-04.3] CursorStore example integration (polling connector)","description":"## Goal
Provide example wiring in a polling-style connector (Telegram/Gmail-like) to demonstrate correct CursorStore usage.

## Details
- Add a reference example in testkit or a minimal connector harness.
- Demonstrate lease acquisition, cursor load, commit, and snapshot.

## Tests
- Integration test that restarts the connector and verifies no duplicate processing.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Example shows the full lifecycle and is copy/paste ready.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:31:50.973236146Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T19:31:50.982909128Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3u58","depends_on_id":"bd-2cqj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:31:50.982849848Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
